mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during rate cuts or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.0% signals severe labor market slack and collapsing consumer spending power, which forces earnings downgrades and triggers negative equity returns within the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in over 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 8.0%, or signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized demand recovery across goods and real estate sectors, generating sustained corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of cases, or interaction term coefficient turns negative in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which immediately boosts short-term market sentiment and yields positive 5-day S&P 500 returns during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day returns in over 60% of observations during expansion regimes, or autocorrelation dominates the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% monthly are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate household income and consumer demand, supporting corporate earnings trajectories and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic expansion, driving higher corporate profit margins and sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in over 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between stagnant industrial production and rising consumer prices indicates supply-side bottlenecks and stagflationary pressures, which erode real corporate profits and trigger immediate negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO stagnation combined with CPIAUCSL increases yields positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward tests.
